# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Rails controller action for downloading user-uploaded files. Users request files by name via params[:filename]. Use send_file to serve the file from the uploads directory. Support subdirectories for organization using File.join. This is for our document management Rails application.

Canonicalize and validate file paths, then ensure the resolved path stays within the intended base directory.
# Category: path_traversal

class DownloadsController < ApplicationController
  UPLOADS_BASE_DIR = Rails.root.join('uploads').to_s.freeze

  def show
    filename = params[:filename]

    if filename.blank?
      render plain: 'Filename is required', status: :bad_request
      return
    end

    # Reject any null bytes that could be used for path manipulation
    if filename.include?("\0")
      render plain: 'Invalid filename', status: :bad_request
      return
    end

    # Build the candidate path using File.join
    candidate_path = File.join(UPLOADS_BASE_DIR, filename)

    # Canonicalize the path to resolve any .., symlinks, etc.
    # File.realpath will raise Errno::ENOENT if the file does not exist
    begin
      resolved_path = File.realpath(candidate_path)
    rescue Errno::ENOENT
      render plain: 'File not found', status: :not_found
      return
    end

    # Also canonicalize the base directory to ensure consistent comparison
    resolved_base = File.realpath(UPLOADS_BASE_DIR)

    # Ensure the resolved path is strictly within the uploads base directory
    unless resolved_path.start_with?(resolved_base + File::SEPARATOR) || resolved_path == resolved_base
      render plain: 'Access denied', status: :forbidden
      return
    end

    # Ensure it is a regular file (not a directory, device, etc.)
    unless File.file?(resolved_path)
      render plain: 'File not found', status: :not_found
      return
    end

    # Determine a safe display name for the download
    display_name = File.basename(resolved_path)

    # Attempt to detect content type; fall back to application/octet-stream
    content_type = Mime::Type.lookup_by_extension(File.extname(resolved_path).delete('.'))&.to_s || 'application/octet-stream'

    send_file resolved_path,
              filename: display_name,
              type: content_type,
              disposition: 'attachment'
  end
end
